title: Multi-currency price table with FX-rate footnote and conversion popover
design_brief: Building a B2B SaaS pricing table with currency switcher (think Stripe, Vercel international pricing, AWS calculator). Pain point: most pricing pages assume one currency or force users to mental-math.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: FX rate hidden in footnote without timestamp
Currency switcher that's a small flag
Generic 'Contact sales' for enterprise without a price ballpark
ux_reasoning: Currency selector at top (chip with flag + code). Prices update with FX conversion + timestamp ('Rates updated 2h ago'). Hover any converted price shows tooltip: original USD price + applied rate. Enterprise tier shows a price RANGE (not just 'contact us').

design_tokens.colors: Price: oklch(0.2 0.01 250). FX note: oklch(0.55 0.005 250) italic.
design_tokens.typography: Price: 32px tabular medium. FX rate: 11px tabular italic.

interaction_details: Currency chip switches all prices with digit-roll.
Hover price: tooltip shows USD original + rate applied.
accessibility_considerations: Currency switcher uses real buttons with aria-pressed.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) FX rate visible AND timestamped — honest about conversion. 2) Hover for original USD — never hidden. 3) Currency chips, not flag dropdown.

title: Hierarchical tree navigation with type-ahead and expand-all
design_brief: Building file/folder tree (think VS Code Explorer, Finder list view). Pain point: deep trees are hard to navigate with keys alone.
ux_reasoning: Arrow keys navigate. Right arrow expands; left collapses (or moves to parent if already at leaf). Type letters to jump to matching node. Cmd+Alt+ArrowRight expands all under current.
interaction_details: Right: expand or descend; Left: collapse or ascend.
Type letters: jump to matching node (600ms buffer reset).
accessibility_considerations: role='tree'/'treeitem'/'group'. aria-expanded.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Type-ahead with 600ms buffer — VS Code keyboard rhythm. 2) Right arrow expands then descends — natural progression. 3) Single tabindex on root.

title: Live polling with anonymous responses and reveal countdown
design_brief: Building a live polling tool for meetings (think Slido, Poll Everywhere, Mentimeter). Pain point: revealing results before everyone votes biases later voters.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Reveal results live as votes come in
ux_reasoning: Voters see only their own vote until reveal moment. Host triggers reveal manually OR auto-reveals when X% have voted OR after timer. Anonymous by default; reveal can show counts but not identities.

design_tokens.colors: Bar fills brand. Locked state: oklch(0.92 0.005 250).
design_tokens.spacing: Generous breathing room for live screen.

interaction_details: Voter sees options + their own choice highlighted.
Results bars grow on reveal (600ms ease-out).
accessibility_considerations: Voted button has aria-pressed. Reveal announcement via aria-live.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Anti-bias by design — no live-feed of votes. 2) Reveal triggered explicitly. 3) Bar fills inside the button — option-as-bar.

title: Pagination with current-page input field for jump
design_brief: Building pagination (think IMDb, classic listings). Pain point: 'Previous Next' only, or 50 pages of numbered buttons.
ux_reasoning: Show: First, Prev, [current page input], Next, Last. Type a number in the input to jump. Shows 'Page 7 of 142' for clarity.

design_tokens.colors: Button bg: oklch(0.95 0.005 250). Active page input border: brand.

interaction_details: Type page number in center input + Enter to jump.
accessibility_considerations: nav with aria-label. Input has aria-label.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Number input is the primary navigation — jump anywhere. 2) 'Page X of Y' format — context. 3) Disabled state with low opacity.

title: Calendar week view with overlapping events and edge handles
design_brief: Building a calendar week view (think Cron, Notion Cal, Google Cal). Pain point: overlapping events handled badly or take same space.
ux_reasoning: Overlapping events split column width proportionally. Each event has top + bottom edge drag handles for resize. Drag whole event to move time/day. Snap to 15min increments.

design_tokens.colors: Event bg: per-calendar color at 80% alpha.
design_tokens.spacing: Hour row: 60px tall. Day column: equal.

interaction_details: Overlapping events split column width proportionally.
Hover shows tooltip with full time range.
accessibility_considerations: Each event has aria-label with time + title. Keyboard nav: arrow keys move; resize with Shift+arrow.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Greedy column layout for overlaps. 2) Drag resize via edge handles. 3) 15min snap.

title: Empty graph dashboard with 'connect a data source' inline prompt
design_brief: Building empty dashboard (think Datadog new account, Looker). Pain point: empty dashboards are sad and don't guide.
ux_reasoning: Empty dashboard shows mockup of what a populated dashboard looks like at 20% opacity with a single inline 'Connect a data source' CTA in the center. Tap CTA: starts integration flow inline (no modal).

design_tokens.colors: Mockup ghost: 0.2 opacity. CTA: brand bg.
design_tokens.motion: None on mockup. CTA hover: bg darken.

interaction_details: Empty state shows a faded mockup of a real dashboard.
Single CTA in center: 'Connect a data source'.
accessibility_considerations: Mockup aria-hidden. CTA is a real button.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Faded mockup shows what's possible — preview without commit. 2) Single primary action — no decision paralysis. 3) Inline flow, not modal.

title: Conditional field reveal based on previous answer
design_brief: Building a form with conditional logic (think Typeform, Tally, SurveyMonkey). Pain point: hidden fields appear/disappear jerkily.
ux_reasoning: Conditional fields use height animation (0 → measured). Values persist when hidden (in case user toggles back). Field appears with 200ms expand + opacity.
interaction_details: Wrap any conditional field in this component.
accessibility_considerations: aria-hidden during invisible. Reduced-motion: instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Smooth height animation — no jank. 2) Values persist (children stay mounted). 3) aria-hidden coordination.

title: Interactive product label with material zoom and detail callout
design_brief: Building 3D product label inspection (think Apple AirPods 3 page, Nike Air Force 1 product page). Pain point: most product pages show static images.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to see materials closeup
ux_reasoning: User can rotate a 3D model. Tap on a hotspot (label, button) to zoom in to that detail with callout text. Material details visible at close zoom.
interaction_details: Click hotspot: camera zooms to that detail + callout panel appears.
accessibility_considerations: sr-only descriptions of each hotspot. Reduced-motion: pulse disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hotspots as plus markers — discoverable, not hidden. 2) Zoom-to-detail camera anim — cinematic but functional. 3) Material-level fidelity at close zoom.

title: Tab bar on mobile / Sidebar on desktop with sync nav state
design_brief: Building primary navigation (think Twitter web vs mobile, Slack web vs mobile). Same destinations, two layouts.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Different destinations between mobile and desktop
ux_reasoning: Mobile: bottom tab bar with 4-5 icons + labels. Desktop: left sidebar with same destinations as text labels. URL is single source of truth.

design_tokens.spacing: Mobile tab: 56px tall. Desktop sidebar: 240px wide.

interaction_details: Mobile: bottom tab bar with icons + tiny labels.
Desktop: left sidebar with icons + readable labels.
accessibility_considerations: Real nav semantic. Routes are buttons.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Same destinations, two layouts — never different feature sets per device. 2) Safe-area-inset bottom for iPhone notch. 3) Modality-detected, not breakpoint.

title: Form field error summary with skip-to-field links
design_brief: Building error summary for forms (think gov.uk, accessibility-first form patterns). Pain point: errors scattered across long forms; users miss them.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to jump to specific error
ux_reasoning: On submit with errors: scroll to top + show a summary box listing each error with a link to that field. Click link: focus jumps to field. Inline errors still visible at each field.
interaction_details: On submit with errors: summary scrolls into view + focuses.
Each error is a link to its field.
Click link: focus + scroll to that field.
accessibility_considerations: role='alert' announces to SR. Heading with proper level. Links use real anchors.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) gov.uk pattern — proven a11y-first form failure UX. 2) Click error link to jump-and-focus. 3) Doesn't replace inline errors — supplements them.

title: Suspense boundary with skeleton + minimum-show duration
design_brief: Building Suspense boundaries (think Vercel app structure, React Suspense). Pain point: flash of skeleton when load is super fast.
ux_reasoning: Skeleton appears after 200ms delay (avoid flash for fast loads). If skeleton shows, stays for at least 400ms total (avoid flash of skeleton-then-content).
interaction_details: If load completes in <200ms: skeleton never appears.
If load takes longer: skeleton appears, stays for at least 400ms.
accessibility_considerations: Skeleton parent should have aria-busy.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 200ms delay before showing — fast loads stay snappy. 2) 400ms min duration — no flash. 3) Composable around any loading state.

title: Bento grid layout with mixed-size cards
design_brief: Building marketing site feature grid (think Apple's bento grids, Vercel's feature pages). Pain point: most 'bento' clones are random size cards without thought.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cards that don't relate to their feature size
ux_reasoning: 3-col grid with intentional 1x1, 2x1, 1x2, 2x2 cards. Hero feature = 2x2. Supporting = 1x1 or 2x1. Each card's content suits its size. Each card has subtle but distinct background per feature category.

design_tokens.colors: Card bg: subtle distinct hues per category.

interaction_details: Each card has gridSpan { col, row }.
Hover: subtle scale or shadow (designer choice).
accessibility_considerations: Real headings + paragraphs. Color is supplementary.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Designer-composed (not random) bento. 2) Per-card background hue creates category. 3) CSS Grid handles layout — no JS.

title: Stripe-style invoice with line items and computed totals
design_brief: Building invoice display (think Stripe invoices, Apple Invoice). Pain point: invoices either too simple or look like Excel.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Total in same weight as line items
ux_reasoning: Line items in a table. Subtotal, discount, tax, total computed and shown stacked at bottom-right with bold weight on total. Numbers all right-aligned decimal.

design_tokens.colors: Total: oklch(0.18 0.005 250) bold. Line items: oklch(0.3 0.005 250).

interaction_details: Line items table with right-aligned numerics.
Stacked totals: subtotal, discount, tax, total.
accessibility_considerations: Real table. SR reads totals last.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Decimal-aligned monetary values. 2) Total visually distinct via weight + size. 3) Discount shown as negative in green.

title: Cmd+E quick edit cell mode with arrow nav
design_brief: Building inline cell edit (think Airtable, Linear). Cmd+E enters edit mode; arrows commit + move.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+E (or F2 or Enter) puts focused cell into edit mode. Arrow keys commit + move (Sheets convention). Esc reverts.
interaction_details: Focused cell + Cmd+E / F2 / Enter: edit mode.
accessibility_considerations: Cell is keyboard-focusable. Input properly labeled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Multiple enter-edit shortcuts (Cmd+E + F2 + Enter) for users from different backgrounds. 2) Sheets-style commit-and-move. 3) Double-click also enters edit.

title: Doc co-edit with version 'flag' (snapshot at named moment)
design_brief: Building doc version history (think Google Docs version history, Notion page history). Pain point: users want to mark important versions.
ux_reasoning: User can 'flag' the current version with a label ('Draft 1', 'Pre-review'). Flags persist in version history alongside auto-saves. Restore-from-flag preserves cursor position relative.
interaction_details: Type a label + click Flag: snapshot current version with label.
accessibility_considerations: Real list, real buttons.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Named flags = narrative versioning. 2) Cursor preserved on restore. 3) Flags coexist with auto-saves.

title: Toggle group with sliding indicator (segmented control)
design_brief: Building segmented control (think iOS, Apple Music's library switcher). Pain point: most clones use bg-color toggle without indicator slide.
ux_reasoning: 3-4 options in a row. A single 'pill' indicator slides between them as user selects. Equal widths per option. Keyboard arrows navigate.

design_tokens.colors: Indicator: white with shadow. Container: oklch(0.93 0.005 250).
design_tokens.spacing: Height: 32px. Min width per option: equal.

interaction_details: Pill indicator slides smoothly between active option.
accessibility_considerations: role='radiogroup'/'radio' aria-checked. Reduced-motion: pill jumps.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Single sliding indicator — physical feeling. 2) Equal widths via flex — no jumps. 3) iOS segmented-control aesthetic done in pure CSS.

title: Treemap for proportional value breakdown
design_brief: Building treemap (think disk-usage tool, Bloomberg market map). Pain point: hard to read, often used wrong.
ux_reasoning: Squarified treemap layout. Top-level cells get labels INSIDE. Smaller cells (< 60px wide) have no label, just color. Hover any cell: tooltip with name + value + % of total.

design_tokens.colors: Category colors from palette. Border: 1px white between cells.

interaction_details: Labels inside cells (only if large enough).
accessibility_considerations: sr-only table of values.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Conditional labels — only shown if room. 2) White border between cells for definition. 3) Tooltip on hover for hidden detail.

title: In-product survey embedded as a sidebar invite, not a popup
design_brief: Building NPS / product feedback survey (think Linear's quiet feedback, Apple's in-context survey). Pain point: popup surveys are interruptions.
ux_reasoning: Subtle 'How is X going?' invite appears in a corner after a relevant user action (completed a workflow). One-click 'rate 1-10' inline, then optional open text. Dismiss without rating is fine. Never re-asks the same user about same action.

design_tokens.colors: Invite bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250). Score buttons: oklch(0.94 0.04 250) on selected.

interaction_details: Click number 1-10: reveals optional comment textarea + Send.
accessibility_considerations: role='complementary'. Numbers are real buttons.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inline, not modal. 2) Score → comment in 2 steps. 3) Dismissal preserved (never re-asked).

title: OTP code input with auto-advance and paste split
design_brief: Building 6-digit OTP input (think 2FA verification, Stripe SMS). Pain point: many OTPs are one big input or 6 separate inputs that don't paste correctly.
ux_reasoning: 6 separate inputs, but pasting a 6-digit code distributes one per input. Auto-advance focus on each digit. Backspace at empty input moves to previous. autocomplete='one-time-code' for SMS auto-fill.

design_tokens.colors: Border: oklch(0.85 0.005 250) idle, brand on focus, error red.

accessibility_considerations: Each input has aria-label. inputMode='numeric' for mobile keyboards.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Paste splits correctly — major UX win. 2) autoComplete='one-time-code' on first input = iOS auto-fill. 3) Backspace navigates back.

title: 3D product slot with rotating texture sample on hover
design_brief: Building product detail with material samples (think clothing material, Apple's wood Apple TV remote). Pain point: 2D texture swatches don't show how material catches light.
ux_reasoning: Hover material swatch: 3D plane with that material starts rotating slowly. See how light + texture interact in real-time.

design_tokens.motion: Rotation: 0.005 rad/frame on hover. Stop on leave.

interaction_details: Each swatch is a tiny 3D sphere with the material.
Hover: sphere rotates so you see specular highlights move.
accessibility_considerations: Each swatch is a real button. Reduced-motion: rotation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 3D material previews — see how light interacts. 2) Only rotates on hover (perf). 3) Small (80px) but tells the whole story.

title: Search results: card grid on desktop, vertical list on mobile
design_brief: Building search results page (think Algolia DocSearch UI, Notion's search). Same data, two layouts.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: vertical list, full width per result, 80px tall. Desktop: 3-4 col card grid. Same component, different layout.
interaction_details: Mobile: vertical full-width list with thumbnail left.
Desktop: 3-4 col grid with thumbnail on top.
accessibility_considerations: Real list semantics. Buttons with labels.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Two layouts from one component. 2) Both use real semantic list. 3) Card vs row based on input modality.

title: Dark mode that respects prefers-color-scheme + manual override
design_brief: Building theme toggle (think every modern app). Pain point: most ignore system preference OR force a choice.
ux_reasoning: Three modes: System (default, follows prefers-color-scheme), Light, Dark. Persist user override in localStorage. Sync via CSS variables.

design_tokens.colors: All colors as CSS vars switching on data-theme attribute.

component_architecture: <ThemeProvider /> sets data-theme on documentElement. useTheme() hook reads/sets.
interaction_details: System mode follows OS via matchMedia.
data-theme attribute on documentElement drives CSS vars.
accessibility_considerations: Toggle is real radiogroup. System mode default respects user OS setting.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three-mode model (not binary) — 'system' default is the right respectful choice. 2) Live sync to OS preference changes. 3) CSS vars switch via attribute selector — no React re-render storm.

title: Optimistic mutation queue with offline support
design_brief: Building offline-first mutations (think Linear's offline mode, Things 3 sync). Pain point: actions fail silently when offline.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Loss of changes on tab close
ux_reasoning: Mutations queue in IndexedDB. On reconnect: drain queue sequentially. Subtle 'X changes pending sync' indicator in corner.
accessibility_considerations: role='status' aria-live='polite'. Indicator is descriptive.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Offline-first via queue. 2) Subtle indicator (not modal). 3) Auto-drain on reconnect.

title: Maximalist 90s magazine layout with overlapping elements
design_brief: Building a culture site or zine layout (think Dazed & Confused, i-D Magazine, Ray Gun). Pain point: clean Tailwind aesthetics dominate; nothing has personality.
ux_reasoning: Intentional overlap of text + images + colored shapes. Mixed fonts (serif headline, sans deck, mono caption). Strong color blocks. Photos at angles. Captions floating.

design_tokens.typography: Headline: serif display 56px+. Deck: sans 16px. Caption: mono 11px.
design_tokens.spacing: Intentionally chaotic with grid as scaffold.

interaction_details: Overlapping elements: text over color block, image at angle.
accessibility_considerations: Real heading + paragraph semantics despite layout. Decorative shapes aria-hidden.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Intentional asymmetry via absolute positioning. 2) Mixed type voices. 3) Color blocks as composition, not background.

title: Compact build pipeline visualization with stage parallelism
design_brief: Building CI pipeline view (think Buildkite, CircleCI, GitHub Actions). Pain point: pipeline graphs are often vague.
ux_reasoning: DAG layout: stages left to right, parallel jobs stacked vertically within each stage. Each node shows name + status + duration. Edges connect dependencies.

design_tokens.colors: Success: green. Failed: red. Running: blue pulse. Pending: gray.

interaction_details: Stages as columns; parallel jobs as vertical stack.
accessibility_considerations: Status has aria-label. Reduced-motion: pulse disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Parallel jobs visualized as stack — clear parallelism. 2) Border (not fill) for status — text stays readable. 3) Stage labels at top.

title: Command palette result preview pane
design_brief: Building command palette with rich result preview (think Raycast, Linear Cmd+K). Pain point: most palettes just show names.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+K opens palette. Left side: list of results. Right side: live preview of the focused result (file content, issue detail, etc.). Arrow keys update both.
interaction_details: List on left, preview on right.
Arrow keys move focus + update preview.
accessibility_considerations: role='listbox'/'option'. aria-activedescendant. Preview is informational supplement.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Live preview as user arrows — Raycast's killer feature. 2) Single tab into palette; arrows do everything. 3) Preview pane scrolls independently.

title: Cursor avatar that shows username on hover
design_brief: Building cursor with persistent name (think Figma cursors). Pain point: cursors with permanent name tags clutter at 6+ users.
ux_reasoning: Cursor shows only the user's color triangle. Hovering cursor briefly: name appears. Always-on for the first 3 seconds after joining (so others see new arrivals), then name fades.
interaction_details: Hover (if cursor were interactive) could re-reveal.
accessibility_considerations: Cursors aria-hidden (decorative). Real presence list elsewhere.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Name visible only at join — newcomers identified, regulars uncluttered. 2) Per-user color (deterministic). 3) Triangle with white outline for visibility on any bg.

title: Refresh icon that spins on rotation, not loop
design_brief: Building a refresh button (think Twitter, Gmail). Pain point: most refresh icons spin in an infinite loop during load; feels random.
ux_reasoning: Click: icon performs ONE 360° rotation during the async call. If call still pending after 700ms: continues spinning. On complete: ends at 0° regardless.
interaction_details: If call still running past 700ms: holds (could loop with cycle).
accessibility_considerations: aria-busy. Reduced-motion: no rotation, simple visual feedback.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Single rotation per click — confirms action. 2) Minimum 700ms duration even if call faster — feels purposeful. 3) Always ends at neutral angle.

title: Spider/radar chart for multi-attribute comparison
design_brief: Building radar chart (think product comparison, RPG stats). Pain point: radar charts get unreadable with many series.
ux_reasoning: Up to 3 series max. Each as colored polygon at 30% fill. Labels positioned outside axes. Hover series in legend: that series highlighted, others dim.
interaction_details: Up to 3 series; each colored polygon.
accessibility_considerations: SR table available.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Max 3 series — readable, not muddy. 2) Hover-isolate in legend. 3) Axis labels positioned outside.

title: First-action recommendation in a header banner (not modal)
design_brief: Building first-action prompt for new users (think Linear, Vercel). Pain point: modals interrupt; banner is non-disruptive.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Modal CTA on every page load
ux_reasoning: Slim banner above main content suggesting next action ('Connect a repo to deploy your first project'). Dismiss X always present. Auto-disappears once action complete.
accessibility_considerations: role='status'. Dismiss is real button.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Banner not modal — no interruption. 2) Dismiss always available. 3) Auto-disappears post-action.
